fixtureOnlyBoundary.detail: The private operator accepts only tracked JSON examples below .agents/skills/bounded-repository-change/examples. The two proved operations therefore establish reusable custody mechanics but not a general user-authored execution request.

sourceCheckoutBoundary.detail: The private operator resolves internal helpers and examples from the Meta-Harness source tree, requires repository Git metadata, and refuses a dirty Meta-Harness candidate.

packageBoundary.detail: The currently published package shape cannot contain or run the D075 operator. Registering scripts/operate-execution-custody.js would be source-checkout exposure, not shipped product closure.

forwardAudit.decisionConclusion: A public surface is warranted, but only if the next gate proves the installed npm artifact can execute one novel user-authored bounded change without source-tree fixtures or Meta-Harness Git metadata.

d076FunctionalSlice.name: Installed-Package Execution Surface
d076FunctionalSlice.productOutcome: A user can install the package, invoke one supported execution command, and receive a durable independently verifiable custody receipt for a real bounded repository change.
requiredBehavior: Run from an isolated npm-pack installation with no Meta-Harness .git directory and no source checkout dependency.
Accept one public versioned execution request containing or binding a sealed approved bounded-change specification; do not require a bundled tracked example.
Execute one real bounded change not present in the two D074/D075 tracked example fixtures.
Prepare exact one-revision shallow authority with no retained remote and no working-tree authority.
Spawn the authenticated agent once, reach VERIFIED, retain the child commit and durable ref, and close normally.
Replay from a fresh process after authorization expiry with unusable execution tools and zero spawns.
Export portable evidence, independently rerun sealed validation, pass leakage scanning, and emit a create-only public execution receipt.
Prove package contents, help text, JSON output, and release checks include the supported command and runtime.
replacementRules: Replace the private operator request/schema and unregistered script rather than retaining dual private/public paths.
Do not preserve compatibility with execution-custody-operator-request/v1.
Do not treat the two tracked DevSpace/Fluxara examples as the public request contract.
Do not add provider, compatibility, concurrency, delivery, recovery, daemon, queue, dashboard, or workflow frameworks.

roadmapDecision.after: D075 OPERATE -> D076 SHIP installed-package execution -> DELETE consumer-led reduction
roadmapDecision.reason: The old DECIDE wording allowed a false-positive source-only wrapper. D076 binds closure to the global npm CLI end-state and one novel user-authored functional operation.

intentDeviation.detail: The direction remains authority-bound execution custody. The aggressive deviation is that public closure now requires installed-package execution and a novel user-authored request; exposing the existing source-checkout fixture runner is explicitly rejected. No backward compatibility is authorized.

nextAction: Implement D076 as one installed-package execution vertical slice. Do not begin broad DELETE until D076 closes under an immutable candidate and retained isolated-package live evidence.
